key_points: Coordinate geometry combines algebra and geometry to study points using coordinates
The Cartesian plane consists of two perpendicular axes: x-axis and y-axis
The point where the axes intersect is called the origin with coordinates (0,0)
Each point in the plane is represented by an ordered pair (x,y)
The x-coordinate represents horizontal distance from the y-axis
The y-coordinate represents vertical distance from the x-axis
The x-coordinate is called abscissa and the y-coordinate is called ordinate
The coordinate axes divide the plane into four quadrants
Quadrants are numbered in anticlockwise order starting from the top-right region
Points lying on axes do not belong to any quadrant
examples: Point (4,3) lies in the first quadrant because both coordinates are positive
Point (-5,2) lies in the second quadrant because x is negative and y is positive
Point (-3,-6) lies in the third quadrant because both coordinates are negative
Point (6,-4) lies in the fourth quadrant because x is positive and y is negative
exam_tips: Always check the sign of coordinates to determine the quadrant
Remember that the x-coordinate is written first in an ordered pair
Points on axes are not part of any quadrant
Use graph paper to clearly plot points
Practice identifying quadrants quickly during exams
